title: GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Pages
content: GitHub Pages lets you host a static site for free from any repository. To deploy, fork a repository, go to Settings → Pages, and choose “Deploy from a branch” (main) or “GitHub Actions”. With Actions, select the Next.js workflow, commit, and run it; the site appears in the Actions “deploy” link. The site URL defaults to USERNAME.github.io/REPOSITORY, but can be changed to a custom domain after DNS setup and verification, and you can enforce HTTPS. The process is available for private repos, and the published site stays public.

title: sa-token + spring cloud gateway 过滤器顺序问题
content: The order of filters in Spring Cloud Gateway with Sa‑Token is fixed by the framework: Sa‑Token’s `SaReactorFilter` is a `WebFilter`, so it runs before any `GlobalFilter`. Thus `Auth` executes first, followed by `TraceId`, `Blacklist`, `RequestContext`, XSS/SQL injection checks, rate limiting, logging, JWT blacklist, forward auth, and finally business logic. Context cleanup occurs last. The supplied list shows relative orders within each filter type, but `Auth` cannot be moved to the 8th position unless it is converted to a `GlobalFilter`.

title: GLM Coding 用户体验
content: The author reviews their experience with GLM coding packages and model switches over time. Initially they used ClaudeCode, then switched to Codex during GLM 4.5, returned to Codex in 4.6, and reverted to ClaudeCode for GLM 5. They concluded that any perceived decline is not a product issue but a cognitive bias. Sensory adaptation raises reward thresholds, and confirmation bias reconstructs memory, leading users to focus on minor flaws and create a subjective sense of product degradation. The upgrades ultimately improve the model.

title: All elementary functions from a single binary operator
content: The article explores how every elementary mathematical function can be expressed using just a single binary operator combined with ordinary constants and variables. It demonstrates that, by carefully manipulating this operator—along with standard function composition—one can reconstruct the full set of basic arithmetic, trigonometric, exponential, logarithmic, and other common functions used in calculus.

title: Optimization of 32-bit Unsigned Division by Constants on 64-bit Targets
content: The article discusses how to efficiently implement 32‑bit unsigned division by constant divisors on 64‑bit CPU architectures. It analyzes existing techniques and proposes optimizations that reduce instruction count and improve performance. The focus is on algorithmic improvements and low‑level assembly tricks that take advantage of 64‑bit word size while maintaining correctness for 32‑bit operands.

content: The article explains how East‑Asian “local” companies—such as TOTO (toilets) and Ajinomoto (seasoning)—have become essential suppliers of critical semiconductor materials for AI, like ceramic electrostatic suction cups and ABF build‑up films. Their legacy manufacturing skills and control over niche components give them a significant advantage in the AI supply chain, making them “keystone” players. Meanwhile, China dominates energy infrastructure and renewable production, further underscoring how foundational, non‑AI industries shape global AI progress.

title: OpenAI也开始恐惧自己训练出的新模型了
content: OpenAI is quietly developing an autonomous cybersecurity product, similar to Anthropic’s Mythos model, that can find, exploit, and chain vulnerabilities. Both companies now limit access to such powerful tools, offering them only to vetted partners because the technology could be weaponised. This shift signals a move from general-purpose models to tiered, security‑focused capabilities, mirroring nuclear‑proliferation concerns. The industry must manage disclosure, access, and control as AI systems enter foundational infrastructure defense and attack.

content: Hieu Pham, a former Stanford‑trained engineer who worked at Baidu, Google, xAI and OpenAI, resigned to return to Vietnam after a severe mental‑health crisis. His departure sparked commentary on Silicon Valley’s all‑night, high‑pressure culture aimed at AGI, where long hours and constant competition erode workers’ well‑being. Similar exits across leading AI firms—xAI, Anthropic, OpenAI—highlight a growing “cognitive overload” crisis, forcing top talent to leave due to chronic stress, ethical anxiety, and relentless ambition.

title: 马斯克的“西方微信”要上线了
content: Elon Musk’s XChat, a new instant‑messaging app from the X platform, launches on the App Store on April 17, 2026. It offers end‑to‑end encryption, no ads, no tracking, and deep integration with X’s AI (Grok). Designed in Rust, it requires iOS 16+, no phone‑number registration, and supports voice/video, group chat, file sharing, and self‑destructing messages. Despite its bold privacy claims, critics note a lack of independent security audits and potential data‑collection conflicts, raising questions about the app’s actual safety.

title: 取代龙虾的是爱马仕？狂揽4万星的Hermes Agent，不只是OpenClaw平替
content: Hermes Agent, a popular open‑source AI assistant, rivals OpenClaw by offering a self‑learning loop that creates and refines skills after each task, automatically storing effective methods. It supports all major OSes, Android via Termux, and multiple messaging gateways, and can use various LLM providers. Its four‑layer memory system prioritizes constant prompts, session archives, skill files, and optional user profiling. Hermes is actively developed, praised for its adaptive capabilities, and positions itself as a next‑generation, evolving personal assistant.
